name: Adelphi University
notes: One of the most decorated D2 programs in the country with multiple national titles. Women's program especially dominant. Draws from the deep New York metro talent pool.

name: Nova Southeastern University
notes: Private research university in South Florida. Strong Sunshine State Conference soccer program with heavy international recruiting from Latin America.

name: Lynn University
notes: Private university in Boca Raton. Men's soccer program is consistently one of the best in D2 with multiple national title appearances and heavy European recruiting.

name: Embry-Riddle Aeronautical University
notes: Aviation and engineering focused university in Daytona Beach. Sunshine State Conference soccer.

name: Florida Institute of Technology
notes: Private STEM university on Florida's Space Coast. Sunshine State soccer with strong international student enrollment.

name: Saint Leo University
notes: Catholic university north of Tampa. Sunshine State Conference soccer in warm Florida weather year-round.

name: Flagler College
notes: Small liberal arts college in historic St. Augustine. Peach Belt Conference soccer.

name: Young Harris College
notes: Small college in the Georgia mountains. Peach Belt soccer with a scenic campus and warm Southeast weather.

name: Georgia Southwestern State University
notes: Public university in southwest Georgia. Peach Belt Conference soccer.

name: Spring Hill College
notes: Jesuit liberal arts college in Mobile. Gulf South Conference soccer.

name: University of West Alabama
notes: Public university in rural Alabama. Gulf South Conference soccer.

name: Auburn University at Montgomery
notes: Auburn system campus in the Alabama capital. Gulf South Conference soccer.

name: Newberry College
notes: Lutheran college in South Carolina. South Atlantic Conference soccer.

name: Belmont Abbey College
notes: Benedictine Catholic college near Charlotte. South Atlantic Conference soccer with access to the Charlotte recruiting market.

name: Catawba College
notes: Liberal arts college in central North Carolina. South Atlantic Conference soccer.

name: Limestone University
notes: Private college in South Carolina. Conference Carolinas soccer.

name: North Greenville University
notes: Baptist university in the South Carolina upstate. Conference Carolinas soccer.

name: Lander University
notes: Public liberal arts university in South Carolina. Peach Belt Conference soccer.

name: Anderson University
notes: Christian liberal arts university in South Carolina. Conference Carolinas soccer.

name: Francis Marion University
notes: Public university in eastern South Carolina. Peach Belt Conference soccer.

name: Tusculum University
notes: Historic liberal arts university in eastern Tennessee. South Atlantic Conference soccer.

name: King University
notes: Christian liberal arts university on the Virginia/Tennessee border. South Atlantic Conference soccer.

name: Christian Brothers University
notes: Catholic university in Memphis. Gulf South Conference soccer.

name: Union University
notes: Baptist university in western Tennessee. Gulf South Conference soccer.

name: Henderson State University
notes: Public university in south-central Arkansas. Great American Conference soccer.

name: Ouachita Baptist University
notes: Baptist liberal arts university in Arkansas. Great American Conference soccer.

name: Gannon University
notes: Catholic university in Erie, PA. PSAC soccer. Strong men's program with European recruiting connections.

name: Slippery Rock University
notes: Public university in western Pennsylvania. PSAC soccer near the Pittsburgh recruiting market.

name: East Stroudsburg University
notes: Public university in the Pocono Mountains. PSAC soccer in northeastern Pennsylvania.

name: Indiana University of Pennsylvania
notes: Large state university in western Pennsylvania. PSAC soccer.

name: Millersville University
notes: Public university in Lancaster County. PSAC soccer.

name: Kutztown University
notes: Public university in southeastern Pennsylvania. PSAC soccer.

name: Commonwealth University of Pennsylvania
notes: Merged institution (Bloomsburg/Lock Haven/Mansfield). PSAC soccer in central Pennsylvania.

name: Fairmont State University
notes: Public university in north-central West Virginia. Mountain East Conference soccer.

name: Concord University
notes: Public liberal arts university in southern West Virginia. Mountain East Conference soccer.

name: West Virginia Wesleyan College
notes: Methodist liberal arts college in central West Virginia. Mountain East Conference soccer.

name: Ohio Dominican University
notes: Catholic university in Columbus. Great Midwest Athletic Conference soccer.

name: Malone University
notes: Evangelical Christian university in northeast Ohio. Great Midwest soccer.

name: Walsh University
notes: Catholic university near Canton, OH. Great Midwest Athletic Conference soccer.

name: Cedarville University
notes: Baptist university in southwestern Ohio. Excellent soccer program that transitioned from NAIA to D2. Known for high academic standards and competitive athletics.

name: Kentucky Wesleyan College
notes: Methodist liberal arts college in western Kentucky. Great Midwest soccer.

name: Davenport University
notes: Private business-focused university in Grand Rapids. Great Lakes Intercollegiate Athletic Conference soccer.

name: Ferris State University
notes: Public polytechnic university in central Michigan. GLIAC soccer.

name: Colorado State University Pueblo
notes: Multiple D2 national championship appearances. Rocky Mountain Athletic Conference powerhouse for both men's and women's soccer. Strong international and Latin American recruiting.

name: Metropolitan State University of Denver
notes: Urban public university in Denver. RMAC soccer.

name: Western Colorado University
notes: Public university in the Colorado Rockies. RMAC soccer in a stunning mountain setting.

name: Adams State University
notes: Public university in the San Luis Valley of Colorado at 7,500 ft elevation. RMAC soccer.

name: New Mexico Highlands University
notes: Public Hispanic-Serving Institution in northern New Mexico. RMAC women's soccer.

name: Colorado Christian University
notes: Christian liberal arts university near Denver. RMAC soccer.

name: St. Mary's University
notes: Marianist Catholic university in San Antonio. Lone Star Conference soccer with a strong mission-driven academic culture.

name: Dallas Baptist University
notes: Baptist university in southwest Dallas. Lone Star Conference soccer.

name: Sul Ross State University
notes: Public university in far west Texas near Big Bend. American Southwest Conference soccer.

name: University of Texas at Tyler
notes: Public university in east Texas. Lone Star Conference soccer.

name: University of Texas at Dallas
notes: Research-intensive public university in suburban Dallas with very high academic standards. American Southwest Conference soccer.

name: Azusa Pacific University
notes: Christian university east of Los Angeles. Pacific West Conference soccer drawing from the deep Southern California youth club pipeline.

name: Point Loma Nazarene University
notes: Christian liberal arts university overlooking San Diego Bay. Pac West soccer in the ideal San Diego soccer climate.

name: Dominican University of California
notes: Small Catholic liberal arts university in Marin County. Pac West Conference soccer.

name: California State University, Dominguez Hills
notes: Public university in the South Bay area near LA. California Collegiate Athletic Association soccer.

name: California State University, East Bay
notes: Public university in the East Bay area. CCAA soccer drawing from the San Francisco Bay Area talent pool.

name: California State University, Monterey Bay
notes: Public university on the Monterey Peninsula. CCAA soccer in a scenic coastal setting.

notes: Large public university in the Inland Empire. CCAA soccer with access to the Southern California youth market.

name: California State University, San Marcos
notes: Public university in North San Diego County. CCAA soccer near the San Diego youth club soccer ecosystem.

name: California State University, Los Angeles
notes: Public university in east Los Angeles. CCAA. Men's soccer has won multiple D2 national championships.

name: Sonoma State University
notes: Public university in the wine country of Sonoma County. CCAA soccer north of San Francisco.

name: California State Polytechnic University, Humboldt
notes: Public polytechnic university on the northern California coast. CCAA soccer in a remote but beautiful setting.

name: Bemidji State University
notes: Public university in northern Minnesota. Northern Sun Intercollegiate Conference soccer.

name: University of Minnesota Duluth
notes: Public university on Lake Superior. NSIC soccer with a strong athletic tradition and scenic northern location.

name: Winona State University
notes: Public university on the Mississippi River in southeastern Minnesota. NSIC soccer.

name: Concordia University, St. Paul
notes: Lutheran university in the Twin Cities. NSIC soccer.

name: University of Nebraska at Kearney
notes: Nebraska's second public university system. Mid-America Intercollegiate Athletics Association soccer on the Great Plains.

name: Fort Hays State University
notes: Public university in western Kansas. MIAA Conference soccer.

name: Washburn University
notes: Public university in the Kansas capital. MIAA Conference soccer.

name: University of Central Oklahoma
notes: Public university in suburban Oklahoma City. Great American Conference soccer.

name: Southern New Hampshire University
notes: One of the largest universities in the country by enrollment. Northeast-10 soccer. Strong men's and women's programs.

name: Assumption University
notes: Catholic liberal arts university in Worcester. NE-10 soccer in Massachusetts.

name: Pace University
notes: Private university in New York City and Westchester. NE-10 soccer in the heart of the NYC talent market.

name: New York Institute of Technology
notes: Private tech-focused university on Long Island. East Coast Conference soccer with strong international recruiting from the New York area.

notes: Public university in New Haven. NE-10 soccer in the Connecticut talent market.

name: University of West Florida
